canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, part-of-speech tagging is defined as the task of assigning a part of speech to a word within a text. This assignment is based on both the word's individual definition and its surrounding context. The single source paper frames this as one of several "traditional NLP tasks". In terms of operationalization, the LinCE benchmark is mentioned as an instrument that addresses part-of-speech tagging, which is also referred to by the abbreviation POS.
papers: Constructions are Revealed in Word Distributions
GRPO-LEAD: A Difficulty-Aware Reinforcement Learning Approach for Concise Mathematical Reasoning in Language Models
Video2Roleplay: A Multimodal Dataset and Framework for Video-Guided Role-playing Agents
Where to show Demos in Your Prompt: A Positional Bias of In-Context Learning

definitions.text: The task of marking up a word in a text as corresponding to a particular part of speech, based on both its definition and its context.
definitions.paper: Constructions are Revealed in Word Distributions

characterization: Based on the provided data, part-of-speech tagging is defined as the task of assigning a part of speech to a word within a text. This assignment is based on both the word's individual definition and its surrounding context. The single source paper frames this as one of several "traditional NLP tasks". In terms of operationalization, the LinCE benchmark is mentioned as an instrument that addresses part-of-speech tagging, which is also referred to by the abbreviation POS.
